retrievedChunks.text: Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants convert sunlight into food.
Plants use chlorophyll to absorb sunlight and convert CO2 and water to glucose.
The light-dependent reactions of photosynthesis occur in the thylakoid membranes.
The Calvin cycle uses ATP and NADPH produced in light reactions to fix carbon.
Chloroplasts are the organelles where photosynthesis takes place in plant cells.
Oxygen is released as a byproduct of the light-dependent reactions.
The rate of photosynthesis is affected by light intensity, CO2 concentration.
C4 plants have evolved a specialized form of photosynthesis to reduce photorespiration.
CAM plants open their stomata at night to minimize water loss during photosynthesis.
The Z-scheme describes the electron transport chain in photosynthesis.
Rubisco is the enzyme responsible for carbon fixation in the Calvin cycle.
Photosystem I and Photosystem II work together in the light-dependent reactions.

finalAnswer: Photosynthesis converts sunlight, CO2 and water into glucose and oxygen using chlorophyll.
